summary: Tested uniqueness check feature for Aadhaar, Mobile, Email, PAN across candidates and employees. Backend endpoint GET /api/candidates/check-unique fully validated. Frontend uniqueness feedback tested in AddCandidateModal, EmployeeEditForm, and CandidateApply public page. All features working correctly end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend GET /api/candidates/invite-links returns HTTP 500 (unrelated to current feature but worth investigating)

EmployeeEditForm initial load: email and mobile show 'Available' correctly with excludeEmployeeId — but aadhaar and PAN fields remain empty (no existing data for RMF0001), meaning those hints only appear after typing. This is expected behavior.
All four fields (mobile, email, aadhaar_number, pan_number) normalized correctly: email→lowercase, pan→uppercase
Debounce (600ms) works correctly - no excessive API calls during typing
useFieldUnique hook properly uses minLen thresholds: mobile=10, email=5, aadhaar=12, pan=10 — shorter values show no hint until threshold is met
test_credentials: admin/Admin@123 used. Known conflict data: mobile 9999999999 belongs to candidate Sunita, email hr@radhyamfin.com belongs to employee Shivani Pathak (RMF0009)
seed_data_creation: Generated a new candidate invite link via UI for CandidateApply testing: /apply/kc8EyMYJgknI88hs-2F2hXtEmElAMs9i
context_for_next_testing_agent: Uniqueness check feature fully tested and working. Backend: GET /api/candidates/check-unique is PUBLIC (no auth). Frontend: useFieldUnique hook with 600ms debounce shows red 'Already registered (conflict_in): Name' for duplicates and green 'Available' for unique values. All three UI entry points tested: AddCandidateModal, EmployeeEditForm (excludeEmployeeId works - own values show green), CandidateApply public page. NOTE: /api/candidates/invite-links returns 500 (pre-existing issue, unrelated to current feature).
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found. All uniqueness check flows work as specified: (1) Backend checks employees first, then candidates. (2) Returns conflict_in (employee/candidate), conflict_name, conflict_id. (3) excludeEmployeeId properly prevents own-value false conflicts in EmployeeEditForm. (4) Frontend shows inline red/green hint with 600ms debounce. (5) Save/Submit buttons disabled when hasConflict=true.
